agency: NYC Department of Youth and Community Development (DYCD)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted the informational site nyc.gov/site/dycd (nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Akamai edge + Dynatrace + AWS ALB) and the DYCD Connect ecosystem at dycdconnect.nyc and discoverdycd.dycdconnect.nyc (Microsoft-IIS/10.0 + Angular SPA with an internal /api/ backend, Google Maps + Places). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Youth and Community Development (DYCD)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 15 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: Informational site on the NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (nginx/Akamai/Dynatrace); the DYCD Connect / DiscoverDYCD apps on Microsoft-IIS + Angular with a private internal /api/
meta.netnew: ProgramApplication — apply to a program (e.g. an SYEP application)
meta.tagline: DYCD already built a public program finder and publishes its whole supply chain as open data — it just never gave either an API, or a way to apply.
meta.headline: The program finder exists as an app but not as an API, and there is no machine-readable way to apply to a DYCD program.

scorecard_notes.platform: Informational site on the shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; DYCD Connect / DiscoverDYCD is a real, custom, DYCD-owned Angular-on-IIS application
scorecard_notes.siteApi: DiscoverDYCD has an internal /api/ backend, but it is undocumented, private, and returns 404 to any public probe — no documented site API
scorecard_notes.openData: 15 datasets, strong on the supply side (program sites 34c, contracts, providers, aggregate demographics 52c) but no clean catalog of the program offerings themselves
scorecard_notes.coreData: Program sites, providers, and contracts are modeled with keys and a full geography spine; 'Program' as an offering lives only inside the finder app
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere — applying to a program (SYEP, COMPASS) has no public, machine-readable surface
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native contract; the finder is a human-only Angular UI
scorecard_notes.ownership: DYCD owns and operates its own finder and portal (unlike rented search); open data is owned via Socrata

headline_findings: DYCD is a split domain: an informational NYC.gov site (nginx/Livesite/Akamai/Dynatrace) plus the DYCD Connect ecosystem — dycdconnect.nyc and the DiscoverDYCD program finder (discoverdycd.dycdconnect.nyc) — an Angular SPA on Microsoft-IIS with a private internal /api/ backend.

DYCD is a funder/intermediary, not a direct service provider: it contracts hundreds of community-based organizations (providers) to run programs (SYEP, COMPASS/SONYC, Beacon, Cornerstone) at physical program sites, and the open data documents that supply chain.

The supply-side data is well published: 15 NYC Open Data datasets covering program sites (ebkm-iyma, 34 columns with a full geography spine, slots, and participants), contracts, contractors/providers, Neighborhood Development Areas, and aggregate participant demographics (k9kq-67vm, 52 columns).

But the program finder is an app, not an API: DYCD already built DiscoverDYCD to help the public find programs, yet its backend /api/ is undocumented and private, and there is no clean Open Data catalog of the program offerings themselves.

Net-new write surface: apply to a program (ProgramApplication) — e.g. an SYEP application — the transaction a young person actually needs, with no public API today; it lives only in DYCD's seasonal online application flow. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 15 DYCD datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DYCD API — supply-side/reference data only (program sites, contracts, providers, NDAs, aggregate demographics).
The DiscoverDYCD program finder is a hashed-bundle Angular app that calls a private /api/ backend (returns 404 to public probes). Uses Google Maps + Places. No public docs, no OpenAPI.
The DYCD Connect ecosystem where providers manage contracts and where program application/enrollment flows live. No public API.
content only; nginx origin behind Akamai edge, Dynatrace RUM, AWS ALB. No content API.
DiscoverDYCD embeds Google Maps JS + Places for site location/search.

fruit.name: Program offerings (SYEP, COMPASS, Beacon, SONYC, Cornerstone…)
fruit.count: ~dozens of program types across 7+ program areas
fruit.notes: The thing a young person actually enrolls in. Authoritative surface is the DiscoverDYCD finder app; its taxonomy (Program Area / Program Type / Service Category) is echoed on DYCD Program Sites, but there is no clean Open Data catalog of offerings and no public finder API.

fruit.name: Program Sites
opendata_match: Evaluation and Monitoring Reports for Program Sites 9f5n-qdib (22c)
fruit.notes: The core supply unit — every funded site with provider, program area/type, service category, slots, participants, and a full NYC geography spine (BBL/BIN, council/community district, NTA, census tract, NDA, lat/long). The richest DYCD asset.

fruit.name: Providers (contracted CBOs)
opendata_match: Provider field on DYCD Program Sites ebkm-iyma
fruit.notes: DYCD is an intermediary — the community-based organizations are the actual delivery layer. Provider name + PortfolioID join sites, contracts, and awarded amounts.

fruit.name: Contracts
fruit.notes: The funding instrument behind every program site — provider, major program, solicitation, funded amounts, term dates, registration. Keyed on Contract Number + suffix.

fruit.name: Neighborhood Development Areas (Service Areas)
fruit.notes: The community-district-level catchments used to target community-development funding (immigrant services, adult literacy, family support). GeoJSON boundaries by borough.

fruit.name: Participant Demographics (aggregate)
fruit.notes: Race/ethnicity and gender-identity counts and percentages per geography/program type — AGGREGATE ONLY. No individual participant record is ever published (privacy by design). Also stands in for Enrollment (slots/participants live on Program Sites).

fruit.name: SYEP for NYCHA residents (Local Law 163)
opendata_match: SYEP for NYCHA Residents by Borough x4x8-m3ds
fruit.notes: Enrollment rollups for the Summer Youth Employment Program among NYCHA residents — the shared surface with the NYCHA domain.

fruit.name: Runaway & Homeless Youth (RHY) reporting
fruit.notes: Local-Law-mandated census and demographics for the Runaway & Homeless Youth program area — aggregate reporting only.

fruit.name: Apply to a program (SYEP application)
fruit.notes: The single most important transaction — a young person applying to a program (SYEP, COMPASS, Beacon). No public API and no Open Data twin; it lives only in DYCD's seasonal online application flow and the DYCD Connect / DiscoverDYCD apps. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.
